title: podman doesn't return 1 as an exit code.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
I'm trying to get the exit code of a container and it always returns 0. Then I decided to try the simplest container to replicate the issue..
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman create --name=test --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'`

2. `podman start -a test`

3. `echo $?`

**Describe the results you received:**
It returned an exit code of 0.

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should return an exit code of 1. Podman version 1.9.x returns 1 but 2.0.3 returns 0.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
2.0.3
```
0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
this is same with docker:
```
$ docker create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
f7933c4c5b5ff6a0c642efe3fad5046a8c1f445911c8f043d9e8ee0641360324
$ docker start f79
f79
$ echo $?
0
$ docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID        IMAGE               COMMAND             CREATED             STATUS                      PORTS               NAMES
f7933c4c5b5f        alpine:3.12         "sh -c 'exit 1'"    55 seconds ago      Exited (1) 49 seconds ago                       hopeful_wozniak
```
podman action:
```
root@develop:~/zgz/podman# podman create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
0be930577c04afbe2bd476e91f676b3180e7aa1cfcd6c82321a1353115bebf9f
root@develop:~/zgz/podman# podman start 0be
0be
root@develop:~/zgz/podman# echo $?
0
root@develop:~/zgz/podman# podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                          COMMAND    CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS   NAMES
0be930577c04  docker.io/library/alpine:3.12  -c exit 1  11 seconds ago  Exited (1) 6 seconds ago          focused_swanson
```

Uhmmm is your docker aliased? 
![Screenshot from 2020-07-31 10-02-28](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/36479528/89019442-34184080-d315-11ea-8a7b-c48722414104.png)

The above image is the exit code when I run on Docker.

just use  the docker,there is my info:
```
[root@centos7 ~]# docker info
Client:
 Debug Mode: false

Server:
 Containers: 6
  Running: 2
  Paused: 0
  Stopped: 4
 Images: 30
 Server Version: 19.03.8
 Storage Driver: overlay2
  Backing Filesystem: <unknown>
  Supports d_type: true
  Native Overlay Diff: true
 Logging Driver: json-file
 Cgroup Driver: systemd
 Plugins:
  Volume: local
  Network: bridge host ipvlan macvlan null overlay
  Log: awslogs fluentd gcplogs gelf journald json-file local logentries splunk syslog
 Swarm: inactive
 Runtimes: runc
 Default Runtime: runc
 Init Binary: docker-init
 containerd version: 7ad184331fa3e55e52b890ea95e65ba581ae3429
 runc version: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd
 init version: fec3683
 Security Options:
  seccomp
   Profile: default
 Kernel Version: 3.10.0-1127.el7.x86_64
 Operating System: CentOS Linux 7 (Core)
 OSType: linux

 CPUs: 2
 Total Memory: 3.7GiB
 Name: copy-of-centos7.8
 ID: GFTC:WMOX:GHAZ:THSX:ETPT:S4JL:ZWKX:ZS5P:7JXJ:HMBQ:NV7T:O3YL
 Docker Root Dir: /var/lib/docker
 Debug Mode: false

 Registry: https://index.docker.io/v1/

 Experimental: false

  192.168.2.112:5000
  127.0.0.0/8

```
there is the result:
```
[root@centos7 ~]# docker create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
Unable to find image 'alpine:3.12' locally
3.12: Pulling from library/alpine
df20fa9351a1: Already exists 
Digest: sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321
Status: Downloaded newer image for alpine:3.12
5f551fd0c8f25bbe47b75720cfa59123d8e8dc799b6c0b653217de3efb98599e
[root@centos7 ~]# docker start 5f55
5f55
[root@centos7 ~]# echo $?
0
```

Okay this is really weird. I'm developing a feature [here](https://github.com/getpopper/popper/pull/874), I need the exit code to be correct to move forward. Let me share the differences:

```[root@li1147-179 ~]# podman version
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
[root@li1147-179 ~]# podman create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:3.12...
  name unknown: Repo not found
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/alpine:3.12...
  unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: Please login to the Red Hat Registry using your Customer Portal credentials. Further instructions can be found here: https://access.redhat.com/RegistryAuthentication
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.12...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob df20fa9351a1 done
Copying config a24bb40132 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
8c734013a267a066f40763ca7e97ef3c61747b1f171d85401aabf844cad55bba
[root@li1147-179 ~]# podman start -a 8c734013a267a
[root@li1147-179 ~]# echo $?
1
[root@li1147-179 ~]# 
```

```[edeediong@localhost popper]$ podman version
Version:      2.0.3
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[edeediong@localhost popper]$ podman create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
10d02605c430dbb7cac5f196a9a7b711f74592268946d2a7bb1ca52e4b79cd27
[edeediong@localhost popper]$ podman start -a 10d02605c430dbb7c
10d02605c430dbb7c
[edeediong@localhost popper]$ echo $?
0
[edeediong@localhost popper]$ 
```

As you can see the podman version `1.6.4` gives a result, but `2.0.3` gives another one. The effect of the second one is I'd have to inspect the container to see the exit code. Like below
```
[edeediong@localhost popper]$ podman inspect -f {{.State.ExitCode}} 10d02605c430dbb7c
1
```

Now this creates an unnecessary complexity when podman can easily give the exit code.  Also, @zhangguanzhang can you add the attach flag to the start command? You're doing a `docker start id` while I'm doing `docker start -a id`. Let's see if you can reproduce the error.

```
[root@centos7 ~]# docker create --entrypoint=sh alpine:3.12 -c 'exit 1'
4dbc2dc4355a754a62a16910a3037578b1efa83d07d63889c1fd773b3c974d8a
[root@centos7 ~]# docker start -a 4db
[root@centos7 ~]# echo $?
1
[root@centos7 ~]# 
```
Yup!! That's it! How do we go about fixing this?
podman also has the `-a` flag，but it has not forward signals
Yeah, I noticed that. Version 1.x series of Podman properly forwards the signals. What do you suggest workaround could be?
I'll take a look at the code and try to commit the changes if I can fix them. Otherwise it's just a matter of waiting for the official developers to fix the problem
Okay... I'd wait around, lol. Thanks so much Zhang!
@zhangguanzhang Thanks so much!!! Now I gotta wait for kubic to distribute it to Ubuntu systems
